jokes.text: You can't expect me to honor what Year-Ago Professor said. That guy was young and foolish.
jokes.setup: Character being asked to honor a promise he made a year ago
jokes.punchline: Treating his year-ago self as a completely different, irresponsible person
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist take on personal responsibility that perfectly fits the Professor's character. The idea of treating past-self as a separate person is original and well-executed.

jokes.text: Can I offer you maybe a nice macaroon cut in seven pieces?
jokes.setup: Customer entering a clothing store
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and absurd hospitality offer
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing character quirk, but relies on stereotypical ethnic humor tropes

jokes.text: Such a stereotype.
jokes.setup: Tailor offering macaroons in stereotypical manner
jokes.punchline: Character calling out the show's own stereotypical writing
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor that acknowledges the show's own lazy writing while still using it. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm looking for something in a space uniform that's respectfully humiliating but can come out of an employee's salary without his noticing.
jokes.setup: Shopping for employee uniforms
jokes.punchline: Openly admitting to wanting exploitative clothing that steals from employees
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the Professor's casual cruelty and corporate exploitation. The phrase 'respectfully humiliating' is brilliant wordplay.

jokes.text: It recycles your solid waste into a nice piece gefilte fish.
jokes.setup: Describing a practical space suit feature
jokes.punchline: Waste becomes an unappetizing ethnic food
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with mild originality, but relies on ethnic food stereotype

jokes.text: Is there a way to just keep it as solid waste?
jokes.setup: Being offered waste-to-gefilte-fish recycling
jokes.punchline: Preferring literal shit to gefilte fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that fits Fry's character. The economy is excellent - maximum disgust in minimal words.

jokes.text: Please don't pull it off.
jokes.setup: Complimenting that someone 'pulls off' a look
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation as removing clothing
jokes.explanation: Standard pun humor, predictable but functional

jokes.text: Yo, Squidward Scissorhands, you got this in an adult robot medium?
jokes.setup: Robot addressing a tailor
jokes.punchline: Combining Squidward and Edward Scissorhands for tailor reference
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture mashup that fits Bender's crude but creative insult style

jokes.text: Well, I am descended from Prince Albert's can.
jokes.setup: Tailor suggesting Bender has noble bearings
jokes.punchline: Robot claiming descent from tobacco can rather than royal lineage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels - noble lineage vs. literal can ancestry for a robot. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: That sounds like something Year-Ago Professor would do.
jokes.setup: Hearing about customer who ordered but didn't pay
jokes.punchline: Using his own earlier logic about Year-Ago Professor being irresponsible
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reinforces the absurd logic while advancing the plot. Shows consistent character development of the absurd premise.

jokes.text: Finally, a uniform I'd be happy to be caught dead in.
jokes.setup: Character expressing satisfaction with new uniform
jokes.punchline: Using death idiom for clothing that will likely cause his death
jokes.explanation: Functional ironic foreshadowing, but the idiom usage is fairly standard

jokes.text: And, boy, does it wick away moisture, gallons and gallons of it.
jokes.setup: Praising uniform's moisture-wicking ability
jokes.punchline: Absurd quantity suggests extreme sweating problem
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation humor that reveals character's gross personal issues

jokes.text: I just wish my fists weren't sewn to my belt.
jokes.setup: Continuing to praise the uniform
jokes.punchline: Revealing a major functional flaw in casual manner
jokes.explanation: Absurd design flaw delivered matter-of-factly. Visual comedy enhanced by Fry's casual acceptance of the ridiculous.

jokes.text: I made free with it.
jokes.setup: Asked if he bought his outfit
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic way of admitting theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's casual criminality expressed through pompous language

jokes.text: Foxhunting is an ancient and noble pursuit that's fascinated me ever since I first heard of it 10 minutes ago.
jokes.setup: Bender defending foxhunting as ancient tradition
jokes.punchline: Admitting he just learned about it moments ago
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of instant expertise and pretentious tradition. The timing (10 minutes) is perfectly specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Look at Hermes's tortoiseshell glasses. The Professor's walrus-ivory teeth. And don't forget the zebra-fur ship cozy.
jokes.setup: Defending killing defenseless animals by citing examples
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and specific animal products
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with visual specificity. 'Zebra-fur ship cozy' is particularly absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: See, Leela, there are two sides to every shameful act.
jokes.setup: Setting up moral relativism argument
jokes.punchline: Openly admitting the act is shameful while defending it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the 'two sides to every story' cliché by acknowledging the shameful nature upfront.

jokes.text: The first thing.
jokes.setup: Bender offering two perspectives on foxhunting
jokes.punchline: Immediately choosing the 'atrocity' option without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction. Economy is flawless - two words that completely shut down Bender's argument.

jokes.text: This is my best protest sign ever, and it was easy because I started with a 'save the ox' sign I already had.
jokes.setup: Leela proud of her protest sign
jokes.punchline: Revealing lazy recycling of previous protest materials
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal about activist efficiency. Shows Leela's practical side undermining her moral stance.

jokes.text: None of your business.
jokes.setup: Amy asking where she got the horse
jokes.punchline: Defensive non-answer implying questionable acquisition
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective implication of hypocrisy - anti-cruelty activist with mysteriously acquired horse.

jokes.text: But he's doing it.
jokes.setup: Hunt master correcting improper champagne fountain drinking
jokes.punchline: Bender's childish logic for continuing bad behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect childish response that fits Bender's character. The economy is excellent.

jokes.text: You're quite welcome, sir.
jokes.setup: Hunt master saying 'I'll thank you to behave like a gentleman'
jokes.punchline: Taking 'I'll thank you' literally and responding to the thanks
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation of formal language. Shows Bender's simultaneous sophistication and obtuseness.

jokes.text: Wow, this is easier than I thought.
jokes.setup: Being shown the caged fox they'll hunt
jokes.punchline: Missing the point that it gets released first
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding humor that fits Bender's lazy, violent nature

jokes.text: Man, it would have been so much easier to kill it in the cage.
jokes.setup: Learning the fox gets a head start
jokes.punchline: Openly preferring murder to sport
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - completely missing the sporting aspect and preferring efficient brutality.

jokes.text: I mean, look at that sign. It's magnificent.
jokes.setup: Hunt master saying he's never seen anything like this protest
jokes.punchline: Praising the sign's craftsmanship instead of addressing the protest
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that completely deflates the protest moment. The unexpected focus on craftsmanship is brilliant.

jokes.text: I used a ruler and I erased all the pencil lines when I was finished.
jokes.setup: Hunt master praising her sign
jokes.punchline: Proudly explaining mundane craftsmanship details
jokes.explanation: Shows Leela's earnest nature being exploited. Her pride in basic craftsmanship is endearingly naive.

jokes.text: Charming. Now, into the ditch with you.
jokes.setup: Being charmed by Leela's explanation
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismissing her violently
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic whiplash from appreciation to dismissal. The economy and timing are excellent.

jokes.text: Ha! I saw that coming from a mile...
jokes.setup: Leela claiming she saw the trap coming
jokes.punchline: Getting caught in trap mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Classic slapstick timing - getting trapped while boasting about avoiding traps.

jokes.text: I saw it coming from a mile...
jokes.setup: Leela repeating the same boast
jokes.punchline: Getting caught in another trap immediately
jokes.explanation: Running gag that reinforces the absurdity, though less impactful than the first instance.

jokes.text: Either way.
jokes.setup: Cheering for the dogs to get the fox
jokes.punchline: Indifferent to the outcome despite just cheering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - showing his complete lack of genuine investment. Amazing economy.

jokes.text: Robot foxhunting is a crime against robo-nature.
jokes.setup: Discovering the fox is a robot
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching sides with robot-specific moral framework
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Bender's robot solidarity. The concept of 'robo-nature' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: They're not foxhounds. They're springer spaniels, you twit.
jokes.setup: Bender criticizing the foxhounds
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction about dog breeds while missing the moral point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the hunt master's priorities - breed accuracy over ethics.

jokes.text: Or rather spring-powered spaniels.
jokes.setup: Correcting about springer spaniels
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're mechanical with spring mechanisms
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that reveals more robot elements. The pun works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Also, can I get my parking validated?
jokes.setup: Making dramatic threat about being a heartless monster
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking about mundane parking validation
jokes.explanation: Excellent bathos - deflating dramatic moment with mundane concern. Perfect comedic timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: and no milking machine should have to milk a robot cow.
jokes.setup: Listing robot animal injustices
jokes.punchline: Including the machine's perspective in robot rights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that considers the milking machine's rights too. Shows the absurd complexity of robot ethics.

jokes.text: Those injustices don't even exist.
jokes.setup: Bender listing robot animal rights violations
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he's inventing fake problems
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that grounds the absurdity. Sets up the next joke perfectly.

jokes.text: Then let's find some that do.
jokes.setup: Being told his causes don't exist
jokes.punchline: Deciding to find real injustices to fix
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - if fake problems don't work, find real ones. Great setup for the montage sequence.

jokes.text: Actually, they was.
jokes.setup: Bender saying robot chickens weren't made to lay eggs in cubicles
jokes.punchline: Chicken correcting him with poor grammar
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - the 'victim' correcting the activist. The grammar adds character personality.

jokes.text: You got to take a number.
jokes.setup: People bursting in and cutting off his hand
jokes.punchline: Treating violence like a deli queue system
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor treating extreme violence as bureaucratic procedure.

jokes.text: You ready to call it a day, or do you have one more score to settle?
jokes.setup: Acknowledging Bender's limited success
jokes.punchline: Setting up return to the main plot
jokes.explanation: Functional transition joke that sets up the climax, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: The second thing.
jokes.setup: Leela asking if he has one more score to settle
jokes.punchline: Referencing his earlier 'second thing' response
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect economy. Shows consistency in character responses.

jokes.text: He's crooked, but fair.
jokes.setup: Judge immediately destroying their injunction
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical assessment of corrupt judge
jokes.explanation: Perfect oxymoron that captures Bender's twisted sense of justice. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: There was a bear.
jokes.setup: Asked where the ice cream went while standing guard
jokes.punchline: Absurdly casual explanation for losing the ice cream
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. The simplicity makes it funnier than a complex explanation would be.

jokes.text: Here. But no chocolate, it's bedtime.
jokes.setup: Crew demanding ice cream as promised
jokes.punchline: Bender treats adult crew members like children with bedtime rules
jokes.explanation: Good Bender paternalism moment. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I spent all night on that.
jokes.setup: Making the pun 'You have been outfoxed'
jokes.punchline: Proudly admitting he worked hard on a basic pun
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's pride in minimal effort presented as hard work. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Sorry, mutton-chump. No fox, no hunt.
jokes.setup: Hunt master demanding his fox back
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response with mutton/sheep pun
jokes.explanation: Decent Bender insult with food-related pun. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: On the other hand, I could run faster through that open meadow. But of course, the forest might protect my fair robot skin from sunburn.
jokes.setup: Bender facing a choice while being hunted
jokes.punchline: Overthinking trivial concerns while in mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender vanity moment - worried about sunburn while being hunted. Slightly wordy but good character work.

jokes.text: Then again, a nice run through a meadow, who could say no to that?
jokes.setup: Still deliberating while hunters approach
jokes.punchline: Continuing to overthink pleasant meadow running
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag but with diminishing returns. Still fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: It's so cute. Horseshoe crab cute.
jokes.setup: Amy seeing the robot fox
jokes.punchline: Uses horseshoe crab as the standard for cuteness
jokes.explanation: Original and unexpected comparison for cuteness. Amy's weird aesthetic standards come through clearly.

jokes.text: And he peed on the locker room floor. That's my territory.
jokes.setup: Listing the fox's destructive behaviors
jokes.punchline: Being most upset about territorial pissing rights
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - among all the damages, he's most offended by territorial violation. Excellent character specificity.

jokes.text: Not Penelope.
jokes.setup: Learning prize chicken was killed
jokes.punchline: Personal attachment revealed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing unexpected emotional attachment

jokes.text: Missed it by that much.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to hit the fox with a stick and missing
jokes.punchline: Uses Get Smart catchphrase for near-miss
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much twist. Character-appropriate for Fry's outdated references but not clever.

jokes.text: Wait, I know what'll confuse those dogs. Catnip.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to throw off the hunting dogs
jokes.punchline: Plans to use catnip on dogs instead of understanding it affects cats
jokes.explanation: Good Bender ignorance moment - confusing which animals are affected by catnip. Character-appropriate stupidity.

jokes.text: Too bad my only merit badge was in interpretive dance.
jokes.setup: Wishing he remembered more Boy Scout survival skills
jokes.punchline: Revealing his only badge was in an impractical skill
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory joke that explains Bender's flamboyant nature while being useless for survival.

jokes.text: Wait. I can use the universal language of dance to signal for help.
jokes.setup: Remembering his interpretive dance badge
jokes.punchline: Deciding dance is actually useful for communication
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Bender finding a way to make his useless skill relevant.

jokes.text: That was my second-favorite ankle.
jokes.setup: Injuring himself while dancing
jokes.punchline: Having a ranking system for his ankles
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd detail that implies Bender has carefully ranked his body parts. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Is that two caterpillars making sweet love?
jokes.setup: Hearing sounds while hiding and trying to identify them
jokes.punchline: Misidentifying hunt sounds as insect romance
jokes.explanation: Charmingly innocent misinterpretation from Bender. The specificity of 'sweet love' adds humor.

jokes.text: And you die first?
jokes.setup: Asking caterpillars to die by his side in final moments
jokes.punchline: Requesting they die before him
jokes.explanation: Perfectly selfish Bender moment - even in death, he wants others to go first. Dark but brilliantly character-specific.

jokes.text: You lousy son of a... I get it. You freed me.
jokes.setup: Bender starting to curse at the caterpillars
jokes.punchline: Realizes they freed him from the trap instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from anger to gratitude. The interrupted curse and realization works well structurally.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. It's Bender's middle finger.
jokes.setup: Finding body part evidence
jokes.punchline: Identifying specifically Bender's middle finger
jokes.explanation: Functional plot joke with mild character humor - of course it's the middle finger that gets identified.

jokes.text: Wait, whom. Who's tripping whom?
jokes.setup: Setting off a trap and asking 'who's tripping who'
jokes.punchline: Correcting his grammar while in danger
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's pretentious side - caring about grammar even while trapped. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: He left an hour before that.
jokes.setup: Explaining when the hunt master left after saying 'nobody leave'
jokes.punchline: He left an hour before even giving the instruction
jokes.explanation: Great absurd timeline - leaving before giving the instruction not to leave. Perfect deadpan delivery enhances the absurdity.

jokes.text: I did. Too bad you didn't.
jokes.setup: Told he should have learned to avoid snares
jokes.punchline: Reveals he trapped the hunt master instead
jokes.explanation: Good reversal with economic delivery

jokes.text: To die in agony at the hands of the world's greatest lover?
jokes.setup: Threatening the hunt master with death
jokes.punchline: Describing himself as 'world's greatest lover' while threatening murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ego moment - even while threatening murder, he has to brag about his sexual prowess.

jokes.text: He was also a robot? Dude!
jokes.setup: Discovering the hunt master was also a robot
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual, impressed reaction to robot-on-robot violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry response - impressed by the cool factor rather than disturbed by violence.

jokes.text: He was also a robot? Dude!
jokes.setup: Discovering the hunt master was also a robot
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual 'dude!' reaction to the revelation
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry casual reaction to major plot revelation. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Let me think about this... I guess it's okay.
jokes.setup: Leela considering whether robot-on-robot violence is acceptable
jokes.punchline: After brief consideration, decides it's fine
jokes.explanation: Good callback to her earlier moral flexibility about robot vs. animal harm. Shows consistency in her arbitrary ethical standards.

jokes.text: I guess it's okay.
jokes.setup: Struggling with the ethics of robot-on-robot violence
jokes.punchline: Giving up on moral consistency and accepting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending for Leela's character arc - her moral absolutism crumbling when convenient.
